title: Echo.
content:
How sweet the answer Echo makes
        To music at night,
    When, roused by lute or horn, she wakes,
    And far away, o'er lawns and lakes,
        Goes answering light.

    Yet Love hath echoes truer far,
        And far more sweet,
    Than e'er beneath the moonlight star,
    Of horn or lute, or soft guitar,
        The songs repeat.

    'Tis when the sigh, in youth sincere,
        And only then,--
    The sigh that's breath'd for one to hear,
    Is by that one, that only dear,
        Breathed back again!
meaning:
Thomas Moore's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Echo."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
